schemes.name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
schemes.description: Direct income support to farmers to supplement their financial needs for agricultural activities.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year per farmer in three equal installments
No land holding limit for small/marginal farmers
eligibility: Must not have income more than ₹10 lakhs per annum
Should not be in profession like doctor, engineer, etc.
howToApply: Step 4: Enter Aadhaar number and other details
schemes.applicationMethod: Online (pm-kisan.gov.in) / Offline at Common Service Center (CSC)

faqItems.question: How often will I receive the payment?
faqItems.answer: You will receive ₹2,000 every 4 months, totaling ₹6,000 per year.

faqItems.question: Can I apply if I don't have a bank account?
faqItems.answer: No, a bank account is mandatory. The payment is transferred directly to your bank account.

faqItems.question: What is the income limit?
faqItems.answer: Your annual income should not exceed ₹10 lakhs. Self-employed professionals are excluded.

faqItems.question: Can tenant farmers apply?
faqItems.answer: No, only landowners can apply. Tenant farmers are not eligible.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.description: Crop insurance scheme to protect farmers against crop loss due to natural calamities and unforeseen events.
benefits: Mobile app for reporting crop loss
requiredDocuments: Land ownership proof or lease deed
Photos of crops (in case of claim)
howToApply: Step 1: Contact local bank or insurance agent
Step 2: Provide land ownership proof and bank details
Step 3: Fill application form with crop details
Step 6: Register using PMFBY mobile app
schemes.applicationMethod: Online (pmfby.gov.in) / Offline at authorized bank branches / CSC centers

faqItems.question: What crops are covered under PMFBY?
faqItems.answer: Crops vary by state. Common crops include rice, wheat, cotton, sugarcane, groundnut, etc.

faqItems.question: How long does it take to get claim?
faqItems.answer: Claims are settled within 72 hours of verification.

faqItems.question: What if my crop is partially damaged?
faqItems.answer: Partial damage claims are considered if loss exceeds 33% of yield.

faqItems.question: Is there any premium waiver?
faqItems.answer: Yes, the government subsidizes major portion of the premium.

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
schemes.description: Credit facility for farmers for cultivational and marketing needs of crops.
benefits: Easy credit access up to 4 times of land value
Interest subsidy of 2-4% on time payment
Loans up to ₹3 lakhs without collateral
requiredDocuments: Land ownership proof or lease deed
howToApply: Step 3: Fill form with land and personal details
schemes.applicationMethod: Offline at bank branches / Online through bank portals

faqItems.question: What is the maximum loan amount under KCC?
faqItems.answer: Maximum is 4 times the value of land holding, up to ₹3,00,000 without collateral.

faqItems.question: How long is the KCC valid for?
faqItems.answer: KCC is valid for 5 years with annual review.

faqItems.question: Can I repay the loan early?
faqItems.answer: Yes, early repayment is allowed without any penalty.

faqItems.question: Is there life insurance with KCC?
faqItems.answer: Yes, there is life insurance coverage for the loan amount in case of death.

schemes.name: PM Krishi Sinchai Yojana (PMKSY)
schemes.description: Scheme to enhance productivity through assured irrigation, covering drip irrigation, sprinkler systems, and watershed development.
benefits: Subsidy up to 60-80% on irrigation equipment
howToApply: Step 1: Check PMKSY eligibility in your area
Step 8: Complete project and claim subsidy
schemes.applicationMethod: Offline at district agriculture office / Collector office

faqItems.question: What types of irrigation are supported?
faqItems.answer: Drip irrigation, sprinkler systems, pipe networks, and micro-irrigation are supported.

faqItems.question: What is the subsidy percentage?
faqItems.answer: Subsidy ranges from 60-80% depending on the category and location.

faqItems.question: Can I install both drip and sprinkler?
faqItems.answer: Yes, based on crop type and water availability, both can be combined.

faqItems.question: How long does project completion take?
faqItems.answer: Project completion typically takes 6-12 months.

schemes.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
schemes.description: Scheme to distribute soil health cards to farmers for balanced and appropriate use of fertilizers and nutrients.
eligibility: Farmers willing to submit soil sample
requiredDocuments: Land ownership proof or lease deed
howToApply: Step 1: Visit nearest Soil Testing Laboratory
Step 2: Provide Aadhaar and land proof
Step 4: Wait for 10-15 days for analysis
schemes.applicationMethod: Offline at Soil Testing Laboratories / Agricultural Extension Centers

faqItems.question: How often should I test my soil?
faqItems.answer: Soil testing should be done once every 2-3 years for best results.

faqItems.question: Is soil testing free?
faqItems.answer: Yes, soil testing and health card are completely free.

faqItems.question: How is soil sample collected?
faqItems.answer: Samples are collected from different spots of your field and mixed together.

faqItems.question: What information does soil health card contain?
faqItems.answer: It contains NPK (nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium) levels and fertilizer recommendations.

schemes.name: e-NAM (Electronic National Agriculture Market)
schemes.description: Pan-India e-commerce platform connecting farmers with buyers for selling agricultural produce.
howToApply: Step 3: Enter Aadhaar and mobile number

faqItems.question: Which commodities can I sell on e-NAM?
faqItems.answer: All agricultural commodities including cereals, pulses, oilseeds, spices, and vegetables.

faqItems.question: What are the transaction charges?
faqItems.answer: Transaction charges are minimal and transparent, typically 1-2%.

faqItems.question: How is payment processed?
faqItems.answer: Payment is made through bank transfer within 2-3 days of sale.

faqItems.question: Can I sell directly without going through APMC?
faqItems.answer: Yes, e-NAM provides direct access to buyers, bypassing traditional APMC.

schemes.name: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan (PMSYM)
schemes.description: Pension scheme for unorganized workers providing monthly pension from age 60.
benefits: Government co-contribution equal to employee contribution
Family pension of 50% on death before 60
No restriction on nature of work
eligibility: Not covered by ESIC, EPF, NPS

faqItems.question: What is the monthly contribution?
faqItems.answer: Monthly contribution ranges from ₹55 to ₹200 depending on age.

faqItems.question: What if I miss a payment?
faqItems.answer: Missed payments can be made in lump sum with penalty.

faqItems.question: Can I withdraw before age 60?
faqItems.answer: In case of disability, early withdrawal is allowed.

faqItems.question: Is government contribution mandatory?
faqItems.answer: Yes, government contributes equal amount to build the corpus.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - PMJAY
schemes.description: World's largest health assurance scheme providing free treatment at empaneled hospitals.
benefits: ₹5 lakhs free health insurance per family
howToApply: Step 2: Apply online or visit CSC

faqItems.question: What is covered under PMJAY?
faqItems.answer: Hospitalization charges, surgery, medicines, diagnostics - total ₹5 lakhs per family.

faqItems.question: How do I find empaneled hospitals?
faqItems.answer: Visit mera.pmjay.gov.in and search hospitals in your area.

faqItems.question: Is pre-existing disease covered?
faqItems.answer: Yes, there is no waiting period for pre-existing diseases.

faqItems.question: How long is the card valid?
faqItems.answer: The card is valid for 1 year and automatically renewed.

schemes.name: PM Awas Yojana (Gramin)
schemes.description: Scheme to provide basic houses with modern amenities to homeless rural families.
howToApply: Step 1: Contact Gram Pradhan or local authority
schemes.applicationMethod: Offline through Gram Panchayat / Block offices

faqItems.question: Can I choose my own contractor?
faqItems.answer: Yes, you can hire your own contractor or take government support.

faqItems.question: How long does it take to complete house?
faqItems.answer: Typically 6-12 months depending on weather and availability of materials.

faqItems.question: Is there any contribution from beneficiary?
faqItems.answer: No, government provides ₹1,20,000 fully. Additional cost is beneficiary's choice.

faqItems.question: Can I sell the house after construction?
faqItems.answer: Yes, after occupancy for minimum 10 years.

schemes.name: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)
schemes.description: Social security scheme guaranteeing 100 days of paid employment to rural households annually.
benefits: ₹258 per day minimum wage (may vary by state)
100 days guaranteed employment per financial year
Equal wages for men and women
howToApply: Step 1: Visit Gram Panchayat or Block office
Step 3: Fill form with personal details
Step 4: Submit with Aadhaar and documents
Step 6: Request work from Gram Panchayat
Step 8: Execute work and get wages
schemes.applicationMethod: Offline at Gram Panchayat / Block Development Office

faqItems.question: How are wages paid?
faqItems.answer: Wages are paid directly to your bank account within 15 days of completion.

faqItems.question: What types of work are available?
faqItems.answer: Agricultural work, road construction, watershed development, and other rural development.

faqItems.question: Can I work more than 100 days?
faqItems.answer: Only 100 days are guaranteed. Additional work may be available if funds permit.

faqItems.question: Is there any age limit?
faqItems.answer: No, there is no upper age limit. Anyone above 18 can work.
